title: describe_image
description: Describe an image for a neurodivergent reader. The caller sends an image URL (or data URL); the server orchestrates a prompt against a vision-capable LLM and returns a literal description, transcribed text if any, key elements, inferred purpose, an alt-text suggestion, AND (v0.2.0+) a `content_translation` — a per-item Input/Action/Goal-style scaffold for structured content (numbered lists, frameworks, flowcharts, infographics). REQUIRES A VISION-CAPABLE MODEL — text-only models will reject the request with VISION_MODEL_REQUIRED. The server stores no image bytes; URLs are not logged.
$comment: v0.2.0 (2026-05-25): adds optional `content_translation` field to describe_image and brief_meeting. The field carries reader-translated facets (input/action/goal/rule/fact/benefit/context) so structured-content images and meeting items become ND-actionable scaffolds rather than OCR-style summaries. Field is OPTIONAL — legacy responses without it still validate. See `.claude-reports/2026-05-25-translate-not-summarize/REPORT.md`.

image_url.description: URL of the image to describe. May be an https URL, a data: URL, or a blob: URL. The server passes the URL through to the configured vision model verbatim. Never logged.

page_url.description: Optional URL of the page the image was found on. Provided so the model can ground its purpose-inference (e.g. a banner on a docs site vs. an email signature). NOT used to fetch anything.

alt_text.description: Optional existing alt text from the page's DOM, included so the model can either reuse it (if accurate) or flag the gap.

description.description: Literal one-to-three sentence description of what is visually depicted. No metaphor, no emotional interpretation, no marketing language. A reader who cannot see the image should be able to picture it from this description.

contains_text.description: True iff the image contains readable text (signage, screenshots, charts with labels, memes, etc.).

transcribed_text.description: Verbatim transcription of any text visible in the image. Null when contains_text is false. Empty string when contains_text is true but the text is illegible — the description should note the illegibility.

key_elements.description: Up to 7 bullet-list items naming distinct elements in the image (e.g. 'red circle, top-left', 'bar chart with 5 bars', 'company logo'). Ordered by visual prominence.

inferred_purpose.description: What the image is FOR, in plain language. Examples: 'Promotional banner for a podcast', 'Diagram explaining the auth flow', 'Screenshot of a settings page', 'Decorative — no information'.

accessibility_notes.description: Optional one-line alt-text suggestion suitable for assistive technology. Null when the image is purely decorative. When non-null, MUST be a single sentence.

content_translation.description: v0.2.0 — Reader-translated scaffold of the image's structured content. Populate WHEN the image contains structured content the reader would otherwise have to decode visually: numbered lists, comparison tables, flowcharts, frameworks, infographic panels, step diagrams. Each entry is one item from that structure (one list element, one panel, one flowchart node). Set to null OR an empty array when the image is purely decorative (avatar, logo, mood photo) — the existing description/key_elements fields carry it then. The legacy fields (description, key_elements, transcribed_text) still fire either way so accessibility tooling doesn't regress; content_translation is the field a neurodivergent reader actually reads when they want to act on the image's content.

eval_corpus_slice.description: Identifier of the eval corpus slice that validates this prompt template. Required so prompt changes can be re-evaluated in CI.

model_provenance.description: Honest declaration of which mode/provider/model handled this request. The cloud-mode banner in the extension is gated on `mode === 'cloud'`.

label.description: Heading/identifier of this item as it appears in the source image — e.g. '1. Emotional Control', 'Step 3: Deploy', 'Risk: high'. Quote source text verbatim where the source provides a label. Free-text where the source uses position-only (e.g. 'Top-left panel').

facets.description: Reader-translated facets for this entry. Each facet decomposes the item into an Input / Action / Goal style scaffold. Use 1 facet for the simplest content (a single rule); use multiple facets when the source provides distinct input/action/goal/benefit components.

kind.description: Facet kind. `input` = the trigger or starting state. `action` = what to do (verb-led). `goal` = the desired end state or purpose. `rule` = an absolute or conditional statement. `fact` = a stated truth. `benefit` = the payoff. `context` = framing or background that doesn't fit the other kinds. Unknown values MUST be treated as `context` by forward-compatible callers.

text.description: The translated text of this facet, written in NeuroDock voice (direct, literal, non-clinical). Per-neurotype addenda may further constrain word count and idiom usage.

lmstudio: any LM Studio model whose card declares vision support

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.x.x while pre-1.0. v0.2.0 added optional `content_translation` and the `$defs.TranslatedEntry` / `$defs.TranslatedFacet` types; legacy v0.1.x responses (omitting the field) still validate. Removing or retyping a field requires a new major version.
